tweets.content: RT by @elonmusk: In the UK, Police officers would have special "cop nights" run by the Pakistani rape gangs where they were allowed to gang rape grooming gang victims as a "Thankyou" for turning the other way. Bring back capital punishment 😡
tweets.translated: RT @elonmusk：在英国，警察会举办由巴基斯坦强奸团伙举办的特别“警察之夜”，在那里他们被允许对团伙受害者进行轮奸，作为对他们转身的“感谢”。恢复死刑😡

tweets.content: The AOC puppet is just flat-out lying

RT by @elonmusk: Being at the launch of Cybercab two years ago was magic. A decade from now there will be millions of these things rolling around the world. The war over autonomous vehicles is on, as everyone in San Francisco knows. But if you add up everyone who has been in ANY autonomous vehicle, whether from Waymo, any of the Chinese, the NVIDIA, any Tesla, it is only a few million people out of eight billion. When I first saw autonomous vehicles at Stanford University about 20 years ago they were so crappy I thought I'd never be alive to see them. I have the first video of the first Waymo on the freeway, and I've been blessed to have been able to watch the development of these up close (got the first ride in Mercedes' AI car, for instance). So many engineers spent so many hours developing them, many going down bad paths. Which happens with innovation. They will save millions of lives. Ford's head of safety told me that what kills people in a head-on wreck is the steering wheel. Now we can see a path to getting rid of the steering wheel totally. Congrats to Tesla and @elonmusk for getting here. Still a lot of work to go and many fights with many communities who will try to ban these to protect jobs. I'm very bullish on Cybercab and will buy one if sold to private citizens, as expected. Why? I never touch the steering wheel in my vehicles already and this will be the lowest cost vehicle with the most safety. And a great big screen and a great sound system to watch whatever I want. When I met the engineers at the launch they said they made many decisions to make it quieter inside, so we can do things like listen to music, video calls, or watch the World Cup. This vehicle is the iPhone moment of autonomous vehicles. One I've been lucky enough to survive the 20 years it took to see.
tweets.translated: RT @elonmusk：两年前参加 Cyber​​cab 的发布会真是太神奇了。十年后，世界各地将有数百万个这样的东西滚动。旧金山的每个人都知道，围绕自动驾驶汽车的战争已经打响。但如果你把所有驾驶过任何自动驾驶汽车的人加起来，无论是来自 Waymo、任何中国公司、英伟达还是任何特斯拉，那么在 80 亿人中，这只是几百万人。大约 20 年前，当我在斯坦福大学第一次看到自动驾驶汽车时，它们太糟糕了，我以为我永远不会活着看到它们。我有第一部 Waymo 在高速公路上行驶的第一个视频，我很幸运能够近距离观看它们的开发（例如，第一次乘坐梅赛德斯的 AI 汽车）。许多工程师花了很多时间来开发它们，其中许多人走上了糟糕的道路。创新就会发生这种情况。他们将拯救数百万人的生命。福特的安全主管告诉我，在迎头撞车事故中致人死亡的是方向盘。现在我们可以看到一条完全摆脱方向盘的途径。祝贺 Tesla 和 @elonmusk 到达这里。还有很多工作要做，并且与许多社区进行许多斗争，他们将试图禁止这些以保护就业。我非常看好 Cyber​​cab，如果按照预期出售给私人公民，我会购买一辆。为什么？我从来没有碰过我的车辆的方向盘，这将是成本最低、安全性最高的车辆。还有一个很棒的大屏幕和很棒的音响系统，可以观看我想看的任何内容。当我在发布会上见到工程师时，他们说他们做了很多决定，让车内变得更安静，这样我们就可以做一些事情，比如听音乐、视频通话或观看世界杯。这辆车是自动驾驶汽车的 iPhone 时刻。我很幸运能够在 20 年的时间里幸存下来。

tweets.content: RT by @cz_binance: 热知识：由于订单簿深度、交易量等原因 币安的交易深度遥遥领先 大额交易在交易所中磨损最低 冷知识：100万刀交易平均滑点 币安是1.67、OKX是4.47、Bybit是7.42 @cz_binance @heyibinance @binancezh mame-inu:native

RT by @cz_binance: If you haven't watched CZ's interview with The Block yet, it's worth your time. 👇 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KdyvngusgtQ One thing I admire about @cz_binance is how clear his priorities are after stepping down as CEO. Instead of slowing down, he shifted his focus to creating long-term impact: • @GiggleAcademy — making education accessible to millions. • @yzilabs — supporting founders and innovation. • Advising governments on blockchain adoption. • Mentoring and investing in promising startups. 😎What surprised me most? He said he doesn't plan five or ten years ahead anymore. He focuses on the next few months because opportunities and technology evolve so quickly. Sometimes, the clearest vision isn't having a rigid long-term plan—it's knowing your mission and adapting along the way.
tweets.translated: RT by @cz_binance：如果你还没有看过 CZ 接受 The Block 的采访，那么值得你花时间。 👇 https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KdyvngusgtQ 我欣赏 @cz_binance 的一件事是，他在辞去首席执行官职务后，优先事项是多么清晰。他没有放慢脚步，而是将重点转向创造长期影响： • @GiggleAcademy — 让数百万人接受教育。 • @yzilabs — 支持创始人和创新。 • 就区块链的采用向政府提供建议。 • 指导和投资有前途的初创企业。 😎最让我惊讶的是什么？他说他不再计划未来五年或十年。他专注于接下来的几个月，因为机会和技术发展得如此之快。有时，最清晰的愿景并不是制定严格的长期计划，而是了解自己的使命并不断适应。

tweets.content: RT by @cz_binance: Binance spends $300M every year on compliance. 1,500+ employees work to keep users safe. Binance stopped $10.53B in potential fraud (2025–Q1 2026), Recovered $114M from hacks in 2025 + $60.2M in 2026 so far. People can say a lot about #Binance But this kind of user protection is rarely seen in crypto.
tweets.translated: @cz_binance 转发：币安每年在合规性方面花费 3 亿美元。 1,500 多名员工致力于确保用户安全。币安阻止了 10.53B 美元的潜在欺诈（2025 年至 2026 年第一季度），2025 年从黑客攻击中追回了 1.14 亿美元，2026 年迄今为止追回了 6020 万美元。人们可以对 #Binance 说很多话，但这种用户保护在加密领域很少见到。

tweets.content: Pinned: POLL: ICE has been abused by the Fake News Media at levels never seen before. They are Great Patriots who work hard, and do a fantastic job in a very hostile environment. Much of this hostility is caused by the Dumocrats and the Fake News. The concept I have had for quite some time — A strong feeling that the name of these Patriots, “ICE,” should be changed to, “NICE,” in that it will totally discombobulate Crooked, Dishonest, and Unpatriotic Reporters and Journalists. For them to say, “We went to a NICE Facility today,” as opposed to “ICE” or, “NICE Agents have deported a Violent Drug Dealer,” they won’t be able to handle it, they will go totally crazy! All it means is adding an “N” (“National”) to “ICE (“Immigration and Customs Enforcement”) — A much more prestigious name. Everyone loves it, but I have been told by the legendary Tom Homan that the Agents do not love it as much as the other population. Who thinks that we should add an “N” to change the name of “ICE” to “NICE?”   Thank you for your attention to this matter! President DJT
tweets.translated: 已固定：民意调查：假新闻媒体对 ICE 的滥用达到了前所未有的程度。他们是伟大的爱国者，他们努力工作，在非常恶劣的环境中做得非常出色。这种敌意很大程度上是由杜民主派和假新闻引起的。我有一段时间一直有这样的想法——一种强烈的感觉，这些爱国者的名字“ICE”应该改为“NICE”，因为这将彻底扰乱不诚实、不诚实、不爱国的记者和记者。对于他们来说，“我们今天去了一家 NICE 设施”，而不是“ICE”或“NICE 特工驱逐了一名暴力毒贩”，他们将无法处理，他们会完全疯掉！这意味着在“ICE”（“移民和海关执法局”）中添加一个“N”（“国家”）——一个更有声望的名字。每个人都喜欢它，但传奇人物汤姆·霍曼告诉我，特工们并不像其他人那样喜欢它。谁认为我们应该添加一个“N”来将“ICE”的名字改为“NICE”？   感谢您对此事的关注！总裁DJT

tweets.content: I've reached 900k followers, thank you everyone! Just some reflection, it's actually a nightmare at higher counts... Which is new to me, since I started at almost nothing earlier this year. 1. Threats of safety, which I don't enjoy. - Random new accounts sending personal threats - Mysterious foreign accounts, sending coercive messages the moment I mention a foreign ticker If I hold an opinion about a stock, I'd prefer not to have anyone show up on the street with a knife at night since it's safety risk at this point... Never felt like I'd have to take that into consideration for holding an opinion. 2. Egregious media narratives - Lot of outlets try to dismiss my ideas like $RPI or $SIVE as "meme stocks" or me as "meme trader" when I talk about supply chain chokepoints or fundamental catalysts to revenue due to AI. Without ever covering the core ideas or hyperscaler mapping I've done. I let success validate my ideas, but it's annoying when they can just pretend their claims like "___ will imminently crash as a fact" which ends up not happening. Or that "___" is just a retail meme stock without mentioning $JBL, Ayar, $GFS or others for CPO scale up or pluggables. I'm grateful to some outlets, like in Belgium for $XFAB coverage, but many others try and paint their own narrative rather than covering a very nuanced thesis objectively. - When I mention a ticker in a foreign market, some try to frame me as some "adversarial account"! I'm just sharing ideas, and I'll always disclose positions/financial interest if I have them. If I say I have none, they just make up some narrative that I'm some foreign institution or random group inside their country that does. Some have even tried hilariously forging screenshots of me to harm my reputation, which was annoying to deal with. Since it's easier to textedit a photo and spread it around than it is to dispel disinformation. Lot of institutions know me personally by now. I just don't go broadcasting my identity to random people in the media/public, since personal security gets expensive and I just want to live about my day. 3. Endless X bot spam - I have endless hundreds of comments a day trying to link external communities pretending they're me. I manually delete, and block thousands of these accounts, sometimes hit regular people by accident. And it's extremely exhausting to the point my fingers cramp certain days. Even if I give the most blatant warnings in bio, people still get scammed and think it's me. Then I get blamed for it too. I actually had a conversation where someone asked if a scammer was me. I said no verbatim, then they double checked like 2-3 more times because they didn't believe I said no because someone had the same username on another scam platform. In another world, maybe we'll get automod type tools. But until then, it's mentally exhausting dealing with all of this. But I do so anyways since I don't want my followers to get scammed. 4. Endless engagement baiting - I have tons of people making up fake stuff about me, tagging me in posts, randomly bashing my ideas while missing any technical nuance in order to get me to comment or farm views. It's a catch 22 since the moment I reply, they get a lot of views, which was their goal. And if I don't reply it just looks like I'm avoiding something (which is not true). I'm very transparent, I always try and entertain people who disagree first. But when it gets into personal attacks or leading questions/disinformation rather than substance then I don't engage. It's a little sad though when people I've interacted with end up caring more about engagement over relationships down the road. 5. People missing the nuance - I share ideas pretty often and I always maintain I don't want people to copytrade or blindly follow, which is why I haven't set up any copytrading apps. Before it was just posting with a few $RDDT friends on ideas like $AXTI, $NBIS, or $ALAB, and people were more knowledgeable traders. Then seeing if markets validate my guesses like with InP substrate bottlenecks. But because many turned out directionally right, to the point it became a central talking point between China/US trade relations, lot of random folks started to follow my account last few months. But many of them miss nuances with float dilution, or material updates along the way such as rate hikes, and I get blamed if a thesis changes. Now since there's so many random people, even if I share an idea about $CBRS last Friday. At market open I had people panicking over a 2% drop, asking me if I did something to a $44B+ company, even if it went up 20%+ today. Now I have compliance review for posts, which delays things a lot but makes sure people don't get the wrong idea. And I have to be careful about my timing, so I post new ideas when markets close. Before, it was just sharing ideas the moment I had them during lunch or in the shower, so this is becoming a headache. 6. I post my core ideas for free. And I've always said this from the start. I actually made my subscribers chat literally by accident, and set the bare minimum amount on purpose since I didn't want to take people's money. But kept it so I could avoid the bots and not spam the main timeline with random thoughts/scrapped research. I think me becoming the #1 most subscribed to person on X was literally by accident. But lot of weird narratives out there when media or others don't believe that I just genuinely want to share my ideas for free. And that people shouldn't need to pay $300 in a paywalled community to see them. _ Not sure if people want to read it all, but just some things I've gone through. It's not as much fun at higher numbers and it would be nice to go back to when I was smaller. Regardless, I'd rather not turn this into a job, or make it feel like one. Will probably take a break when I hit 1M as a milestone and do something else that will help out the community like sharing my research pipelines and such. Think a lot of my friends at 10-50K follower counts, seem to experience some of these issues too. But problems get magnified a lot at this level. I never needed the X creator fees or anything, just wanted to share ideas for fun if people wanted to listen. And didn't expect any popularity, but it's nice to see that people find my ideas compelling.
tweets.translated: 我的粉丝已经达到90万了，谢谢大家！只是一些反思，这实际上是更高计数的噩梦......这对我来说是新的，因为今年早些时候我几乎一无所有。 1. 安全威胁，我不喜欢。 - 随机新账户发送人身威胁 - 神秘的外国账户，在我提到外国股票时发送强制信息 如果我对一只股票持有意见，我宁愿不要有人在晚上拿着刀出现在街上，因为此时存在安全风险......从来没有觉得我必须考虑到这一点才能持有意见。 2. 恶劣的媒体叙述——当我谈论人工智能带来的供应链瓶颈或收入的基本催化剂时，许多媒体试图将我的想法（如 $RPI 或 $SIVE）视为“模因股票”，或将我视为“模因交易员”。没有涵盖我所做的核心思想或超大规模映射。我让成功验证了我的想法，但当他们假装他们的主张“___将立即崩溃”而最终没有发生时，这很烦人。或者“___”只是零售 m​​eme 股票，没有提及 $JBL、Ayar、$GFS 或其他用于 CPO 扩展或可插入的内容。我很感谢一些媒体，例如比利时的 $XFAB 报道，但许多其他媒体试图描绘自己的叙述，而不是客观地报道非常微妙的论文。 - 当我提到国外市场的股票时，有些人试图将我视为“敌对账户”！我只是分享想法，如果有的话，我总是会披露职位/财务利益。如果我说我没有，他们只是编造一些故事，说我是他们国家的某个外国机构或随机团体。有些人甚至试图搞笑地伪造我的屏幕截图来损害我的声誉，这很难处理。因为用文字编辑照片并将其传播比消除虚假信息更容易。现在很多机构都认识我了。我只是不会向媒体/公众中的随机人广播我的身份，因为个人安全变得昂贵，而且我只想过好自己的一天。 3. 无尽的 X 机器人垃圾邮件 - 我每天都会有无数条评论试图链接外部社区，假装他们是我。我手动删除并屏蔽了数千个这样的帐户，有时会不小心碰到普通人。而且有几天我的手指都抽筋了，真是太累了。即使我在生物中给出了最明目张胆的警告，人们仍然会被骗并认为是我。然后我也因此受到指责。事实上，我有过一次谈话，有人问我是不是骗子。我一字不差地说不，然后他们又仔细检查了 2-3 次，因为他们不相信我说不，因为有人在另一个诈骗平台上有相同的用户名。在另一个世界中，也许我们会得到 automod 类型的工具。但在那之前，处理所有这些都会让人精神疲惫。但无论如何我都会这样做，因为我不想让我的追随者被骗。 4.无休止的参与诱饵——有很多人编造关于我的虚假内容，在帖子中标记我，随机攻击我的想法，却忽略了任何技术上的细微差别，以便让我发表评论或表达观点。这是第 22 条军规，因为从我回复的那一刻起，他们就得到了很多浏览量，这就是他们的目标。如果我不回复，看起来就像我在逃避某些事情（这不是真的）。我很透明，我总是先尝试并招待那些不同意的人。但当它涉及人身攻击或引导性问题/虚假信息而不是实质内容时，我就不会参与。不过，当与我交往过的人最终更关心参与度而不是未来的关系时，这有点令人难过。 5. 人们忽略了细微差别 - 我经常分享想法，而且我始终坚称我不希望人们复制交易或盲目跟随，这就是为什么我没有设置任何复制交易应用程序。在此之前，它只是与一些 $RDDT 朋友发布关于 $AXTI、$NBIS 或 $ALAB 等想法的文章，而且人们都是知识渊博的交易者。然后看看市场是否验证了我的猜测，就像 InP 衬底瓶颈一样。但因为很多人的方向是正确的，以至于它成为中美贸易关系之间的一个中心话题，很多随机的人在过去几个月开始关注我的账户。但他们中的许多人错过了浮动稀释的细微差别，或者一路上的材料更新（例如加息），如果论文发生变化，我就会受到指责。现在既然有这么多随机的人，即使我上周五分享了一个关于 $CBRS 的想法。开盘时，人们对 2% 的下跌感到恐慌，问我是否对一家 44B+ 美元的公司做了什么，即使它今天上涨了 20%+。现在我对帖子进行合规性审查，这会延迟很多事情，但可以确保人们不会产生错误的想法。我必须小心我的时机，所以我会在市场收盘时发布新的想法。以前，我只是在午餐或淋浴时分享想法，所以这变得令人头痛。 6.我免费发布我的核心想法。我总是从工作人员那里说这句话

tweets.content: For robotics companies, I have a favorable view on Unitree and Agility Robotics, two humanoid players. And I have largest concentration in Agility Robotics, since I personally prefer US humanoid players. For upstream component exposure, I currently own: - Harmonic Drive (6324) given high content BOM on things like harmonic reduction gear. - Vishay Precision for sensors and a possible candidate for Telsa Optimus. LeaderDrive (688017) and Schaeffler I have favorable views on but don't own personally. Many of the other AI DC players, I have indirect exposure to robotics like memory. Don't recommend anyone to copy, just sharing personal positions/thoughts. Humanoid sector is large, and as seen with Goldman Sachs report that "Korean companies will command a 30% direct and indirect share of global humanoid robot production". Lot of players out there globally. This was an older report but just linking it again since you see all of them pop up in GS institutional reports + what they cover. Agility Robotics is my personal favorite as of now.
tweets.translated: 对于机器人公司，我看好Unitree和Agility Robotics这两家人形机器人公司。我最关注的是 Agility Robotics，因为我个人更喜欢美国的类人机器人玩家。对于上游组件暴露，我目前拥有： - Harmonic Drive (6324)，给出了谐波减速齿轮等高内容 BOM。 - Vishay Precision 传感器和 Telsa Optimus 的可能候选者。我对LeaderDrive (688017) 和舍弗勒有好感，但个人并不认同。许多其他 AI DC 玩家，我都间接接触过像记忆这样的机器人技术。不建议任何人复制，仅分享个人立场/想法。人形机器人行业规模庞大，高盛报告称“韩国公司将直接和间接占据全球人形机器人生产的 30% 份额”。全球有很多玩家。这是一份较旧的报告，但只是再次链接它，因为您会看到所有这些报告都出现在 GS 机构报告中以及它们所涵盖的内容。到目前为止，Agility Robotics 是我个人最喜欢的。

tweets.content: Japan's Rakuten to establish joint venture with $ASTS to build out LEO satellite networks for Japan per Digitimes/Nikkei. "The move is widely viewed as a strategic response to the growing influence of $SPCX Starlink in Japan"
tweets.translated: 据 Digitimes/Nikkei 报道，日本乐天公司将与 $ASTS 成立合资企业，为日本建设 LEO 卫星网络。 “此举被广泛视为对 $SPCX Starlink 在日本日益增长的影响力的战略回应”
